briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 28, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Policies and Expanded Citizen Participation
sections.content: Domestically, public discourse has officially begun to gather citizen opinions ahead of the revision of the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act [1]. The National Assembly's Special Committee on Climate Change and the Carbon Neutrality Act Revision Public Deliberation Committee held its first large-scale citizen debate, encouraging public participation [2, 4]. This, along with the existence of the Presidential Committee on National Climate Crisis Response, is a positive step that demonstrates the nation's commitment to addressing the climate crisis and the importance of citizen involvement [3]. Such efforts to incorporate citizens' voices into the legislative revision process are laying a crucial foundation for achieving carbon neutrality goals [1].

sections.title: 🌱 Regional Spread of Domestic Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Across South Korea, efforts to transition to renewable energy are actively unfolding at the regional level. In Gyeongju, Gyeongsangbuk-do, the attraction of the first i-SMR (small modular reactor) unit is gaining momentum, accelerating the steel industry's response to carbon neutrality [5]. Furthermore, Pyeongchang County has begun accepting applications for its 2026 renewable energy housing support project, aiding the energy transition in the residential sector [8], and Haenam County has launched a public-private consultative body to create a renewable energy self-sufficient city [10]. In addition, events such as the Daegu Regional Environmental Office's renewable energy council meeting and the visit of the Muan County Mayor to a village solar power plant demonstrate sustained interest and efforts in expanding renewable energy within local communities [7, 11].

sections.title: 🌍 Challenges and Opportunities in the International Clean Energy Industry
sections.content: The international clean energy industry is exhibiting a complex landscape where opportunities and challenges coexist. A forecast suggests that during a second Trump term, renewable energy generation in the U.S. will experience rapid growth, heralding the 'golden age' of ESS (Energy Storage System) batteries [6]. At Huawei's 6th Global Installer Summit, leading installers from around the world gathered to discuss the future and seize renewable energy opportunities in the AI era [9]. However, there are also reports that clean energy companies are struggling to survive in the current 'Trump era,' indicating that policy uncertainties could impact industrial growth [17].

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Global Climate Change Impacts and Warnings
sections.content: The impacts of climate change have now reached an undeniable level and are intensifying globally in various forms [14]. Research has been published indicating difficulties in tracking the effects of climate change on mental health [12], and the phenomenon of invasive plants migrating to new areas is also being observed [15]. In the Pacific Northwest, climate change is posing challenges to avalanche forecasting [16], and direct impacts are also anticipated in the agricultural sector, with cotton production, for instance, being threatened [18]. In Wisconsin, ice anglers are concerned that their traditions may disappear due to climate change [21], and in the Gainesville area of Florida, widespread changes are being detected across society, including an anticipated population increase as climate change progresses [13]. News that a dream about polar bears offered important lessons about climate change serves as another reminder of the severity of this situation [20].

sections.title: ⚠️ Policy Recommendations and the Importance of Information for Climate Change Response
sections.content: Policy recommendations and the importance of accurate information are being emphasized to address the deepening climate crisis. A climate change expert warned that a carbon tax increase is essential, stressing the need for immediate policy changes [22]. Furthermore, an Australian Senate report stated that misinformation about climate change must be actively countered, indicating the importance of efforts to ensure public understanding [19]. These warnings and reports from experts demonstrate the need for a multifaceted approach to climate crisis response [19, 22].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has clearly demonstrated both domestic and international efforts to address climate change, alongside the escalating real threats posed by a deepening climate crisis. This is a time that calls for continuous attention and action. This concludes today's climate briefing.
